host.kernel_cmdline: BOOT_IMAGE=/boot/vmlinuz-6.15.11-061511-generic root=UUID=85355174-a2ad-4e84-bf78-53fac30b89fb ro panic=10 panic_on_oops=1 softlockup_panic=1 hung_task_panic=1 splash quiet iommu=pt vt.handoff=7

benchmarks.name: map_lookup_churn
benchmarks.description: Repeated input_map lookups over a fixed array payload

benchmarks.name: map_roundtrip
benchmarks.description: Repeated lookup/update roundtrips through an auxiliary array map

benchmarks.name: hash_map_lookup
benchmarks.description: Repeated hash-map lookups over a generated key set
benchmarks.hypothesis: Hash buckets should expose lookup overhead beyond the array-backed baseline

benchmarks.name: percpu_map_update
benchmarks.description: Repeated percpu-array updates over generated key-value pairs
benchmarks.hypothesis: Per-CPU updates should capture current-CPU slot selection plus helper update cost

benchmarks.name: helper_call_1
benchmarks.description: Single helper invocation in a staged-input runtime path
benchmarks.hypothesis: A single helper isolates the floor cost of helper dispatch and ABI setup

benchmarks.name: helper_call_10
benchmarks.description: Ten helper invocations in a staged-input runtime path
benchmarks.hypothesis: Repeated helpers should expose how runtime helper dispatch scales beyond the floor case

benchmarks.name: helper_call_100
benchmarks.description: One hundred helper invocations in a staged-input runtime path
benchmarks.hypothesis: Helper-heavy programs should reveal runtime behavior closer to the BCF corpus tail

benchmarks.name: probe_read_heavy
benchmarks.description: Repeated bpf_probe_read_kernel calls against a staged map-backed source
benchmarks.hypothesis: Probe-read-heavy loops should amplify helper dispatch plus kernel-memory copy cost

benchmarks.name: get_time_heavy
benchmarks.hypothesis: Dense ktime helper traffic isolates repeated helper dispatch in a fixed loop
